description:
⚡ React canvas game tutorial: Tap Football is a side-scrolling physics arcade game with 10 themed stages, a goalkeeper, and zero game libraries — just React 18, TypeScript, and the HTML5 Canvas API. Swipe to launch a soccer ball, tap to bounce it through emoji obstacles, and thread past a patrolling goalkeeper to clear all 10 worlds.

The whole engine fits in a single engine.ts file running at 60 FPS. What makes it interesting is how little code creates ten completely different-feeling stages: three formula lines in levels.ts set scroll speed, obstacle count, and goal distance, while an emoji array and a background index swap the scenery. No sprite sheets, no physics library, no game framework.


🎯 What you'll learn

How to run a custom requestAnimationFrame game loop inside a React component
Physics from scratch: gravity 0.15, bounce 0.7, drag 0.99 — all pure math
Data-driven level design: one config object per stage, ten worlds from one engine
React screen-state machine (START / PLAYING / LEVEL_CLEAR / GAME_OVER / GAME_COMPLETE)
Circle-vs-rectangle collision detection on emoji bounding boxes
Shipping a Canvas game as an installable PWA with vite-plugin-pwa
